library_preparation_preflight.summary: Pekko HTTP's normal server/client APIs are built on Pekko Streams and an ActorSystem, but the resolved artifact declares pekko-stream as provided; official usage docs require adding Pekko Stream and an actor dependency alongside pekko-http.

deterministic_setup.reason: Required for meaningful Pekko HTTP server/client usage; the pekko-http_2.13:1.3.0 POM marks pekko-stream_2.13:1.1.5 as provided, so it is not available from the normal target-library dependency alone.
Official Pekko HTTP examples create a typed ActorSystem and recommend adding pekko-actor-typed with pekko-stream and pekko-http.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property appears necessary. Tests should create a local ActorSystem, bind only to localhost using an ephemeral port when starting a server, avoid stdin/fixed-port examples, and always unbind and terminate the ActorSystem in cleanup/finally code.
risks: If tests leave the ActorSystem running, Gradle/native test execution can hang due to Pekko threads.
Scala/Pekko native-image behavior may still require metadata adjustments; local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
